description: A destructuring `for [:x :y] in <expr>` binder (spec/grammar.md:127,136-137) lives only within its own loop body: reads of `:x`/`:y` inside the body are fine, but reading `:x` again after `end for` has no visible declaration in scope, so it raises `ol-undefined-var` (spec/execution-model.md:317-327). This is the destructuring-specific case of the binder-not-leaked rule — the existing `core-language/check/undefined-var-binder-not-leaked` fixture only covers a bare-name `for ... from ... to` binder.
